question: Will CPI rise more than 1.3% in March 2026?
reasoning: The March 2026 CPI data has already been officially released by the Bureau of Labor Statistics on April 10, 2026, showing a 0.3% monthly increase. This is significantly below the 1.3% threshold required for a YES resolution. The market should definitively resolve to NO, making the true probability 0%. However, the current 2% market price suggests some participants may not be aware the data has been released.
key_factors: Official BLS data already released showing 0.3% monthly increase
Result is far below the 1.3% threshold
Market closes April 10 at 12:25 PM, data was released at 8:30 AM same day
risks: Market inefficiency due to timing of data release vs market close
